text: Match each addressing mode (P: Immediate, Q: Indirect, R: Base with index, S: Base with offset/displacement) with its data element (1: Array element, 2: Pointer, 3: Record element, 4: Constant).
Consider a processor with load-store architecture. First operand is always destination. Which sequence corresponds to Z = X + Y? (X,Y,Z memory, R0,R1,R2 registers)

options.B: LOAD R0, X; ADD Z, R0, Y
options.C: ADD R0, X, Y; STORE Z, R0
options.D: LOAD R0, X; LOAD R1, Y; ADD R2, R0, R1; STORE Z, R2

text: Which dependency among register operands of different instructions can cause a data hazard in a pipelined processor?
Consider Boolean expression F(P,Q) = (P̅+Q) ⊕ (P̅Q). Which is equivalent to F?
Consider 8-bit signed integers X and Y in sign-magnitude form. X: 1 0110100, Y: 01001100. Which operation(s) to compute Z result in arithmetic overflow?
Consider C statements: char *str1 = "Hello; (S1); char *str2 = "Hello"; (S2); int *str3 = "Hello"; (S3). Which is/are correct?

options.A: S1 and S2 have syntactic errors
options.B: S2 has a lexical error and S3 has a syntactic error
options.C: S1 has a lexical error and S3 has a semantic error
options.D: S1 has a syntactic error and S3 has a semantic error

text: Which statement(s) about LL(1) parsers is/are true?

options.B: For a grammar to be LL(1), it must be left-recursive
options.C: For a grammar to be LL(1), it must be left-factored
options.D: LL(1) parsers are more powerful than SLR parsers

text: Consider: int bar(int n){ if(n==1) return 0; else return 1+bar(n/2); } int foo(int n){ if(n==1) return 1; else return 1+foo(bar(n)); } The smallest positive integer n for which foo(n) returns 5 is ______. (answer in integer)
